title: Logical Operators 🧠
description: Combine conditions using AND, OR, and NOT.

slides.title: Combining Rules 🧩
slides.content:
What if you need multiple things to be true at the same time? 

e.g., A user can enter if they are *over 18* **AND** they have a *ticket*.

We do this with **Logical Operators**.

slides.title: The AND Operator (`&&`) 🤝
slides.content:
## AND (`&&`)

We use two ampersands `&&` to combine checks.

`if (age > 18 && hasTicket == true)`

The entire `if` block will only run if **BOTH** sides are true.

quizData.question: If we have `if (true && false)`, what is the final result?
quizData.explanation: Because one side is false, the entire AND operation becomes false. BOTH must be true.

slides.title: The OR Operator (`||`) 🔀
slides.content:
## OR (`||`)

We use two pipe characters `||` (usually the key above Enter) to mean OR.

`if (isWeekend == true || isHoliday == true)`

The block will run if **EITHER** side is true (or if both are true).

quizData.question: If we have `if (false || true)`, what is the final result?
quizData.explanation: With an OR (`||`), as long as AT LEAST ONE side is true, the whole expression is true.

slides.title: The NOT Operator (`!`) 🚫
slides.content:
## NOT (`!`)

We already saw `!=` (Not Equal). The exclamation mark `!` can be put in front of any boolean value to flip it to its opposite.

`!true` becomes `false`.
`!false` becomes `true`.

slides.title: Putting it in Action ✨
slides.content: Watch the NOT operator flip the value of `isSleeping`.
